name: Red Pepper Flatbread
recipeIngredient: 1/4 cup Olive oil or vegetable oil
recipeInstructions:
Do not use self-rising flour in this recipe.
Mix 1 cup of the flour, sugar, salt, and yeast in a large bowl. Add olive oil and warm water. Beat on medium speed for 3 minutes, scraping the bowl occasionally. Stir in enough remaining flour until the dough is soft and leaves side of the bowl. Turn onto lightly floured surface.
Knead 5 to 10 minutes or until dough is smooth and elastic. Place in a greased bowl and turn greased side up. Cover and let rise in warm place for 1 to 1-1/2 hours, or until doubled.
Heat oven to 425°F. Grease 2 cookie sheets. Punch down dough and divide into half. Shape each half into flattened 12-inch round on a cookie sheet. Cover and let rise in warm place for 20 minutes. Prick centers and 1 inch from edge thoroughly with fork. Brush with oil. Sprinkle each with 1 tablespoon cheese.
Bake 12 to 15 minutes, or until golden brown. Cook and stir red bell peppers and onions in 1 tablespoon olive oil or vegetable oil in a 10-inch skillet over medium heat until softened. Arrange on flatbreads. (2 FLATBREADS; 85 CALORIES PER PIECE.)
